demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; usually independent

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I cannot stop this tickle in my throat; I cough until I gag, and I actually passed out earlier from the effort. (Spouse present, made the call.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The coughing fits got so violent tonight that he briefly fainted and couldn't catch his breath or speak — we were frightened and didn't want to wait until morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about two months ago, shortly after a mild head-cold
symptom_narrative.duration: more than 8 weeks of a constant dry cough, much worse tonight
symptom_narrative.character: a relentless dry, tickly, scratchy feeling in the throat that sets off violent coughing fits with gagging and retching
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's ever been tonight — coughed so hard he gagged, retched, and then blacked out for a moment
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in unpredictable fits through the day and evening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped — a hard swallow and a sip of water sometimes eases it briefly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: just talking, laughing, or breathing cool air sets off a fit; the room being cool tonight is a trigger
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: hoarse, croaky voice for a while after each bad fit; feels wiped out and a bit shaky after tonight's faint
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the cough has been constant for two months but he has never fainted from it before tonight
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually independent; tonight exhausted and frightened to talk in case it triggers another fit; has been avoiding seeing people because the coughing is embarrassing and unpredictable

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a lingering cough ever since a cold a couple of months ago that just won't settle
past_medical_history.how_managed: has tried a few treatments from the doctor but none worked

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: tablets for reflux and a nasal spray were tried earlier but didn't help

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: retired, lives with spouse who witnessed tonight's faint; has been withdrawing socially because of the unpredictable coughing

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says he was mid-coughing-fit, went limp and 'passed out' for a few seconds, then came round on his own and was groggy but talking
objective_data_offered.timing: this evening, during a severe coughing fit
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander account of a brief loss of consciousness; duration and whether there was any jerking or tongue-biting not reliably established over chat

objective_data_offered.value: appears exhausted, sitting still and clutching a glass of water, speaking very slowly and softly to avoid setting off a cough; voice sounds hoarse
between the fits he says his breathing feels normal and he can talk in full sentences; it's only during a fit that he can't catch his breath
the room is cool tonight, which he identifies as setting off the cough
